parameters.responseBody: ={{ { success: false, error: 'Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.', statusCode: 429 } }}

parameters.query: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.price, p.sku, i.quantity, i.reserved FROM products p LEFT JOIN inventory i ON p.id = i.product_id WHERE p.id = ANY($1) AND p.is_active = true AND p.company_id = $2

parameters.value: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, invoiceNumber: $json.invoice_number, totalAmount: $json.final_total, message: 'Sale registered successfully' }) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { success: true, invoiceNumber: $json.invoice_number, totalAmount: $json.final_total, message: 'Sale registered successfully' } }}
={{ { success: true, message: 'Report generation started', jobId: $execution.id, status: 'processing' } }}

parameters.query: SELECT s.id, s.invoice_number, s.total_amount, s.final_total, s.payment_method, s.created_at, u.profile->>'name' as seller_name, c.name as customer_name FROM sales s LEFT JOIN users u ON s.user_id = u.id LEFT JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.id WHERE s.company_id = $1 AND s.created_at >= $2 AND s.created_at <= $3 ORDER BY s.created_at DESC LIMIT $4 OFFSET $5

parameters.arguments: -c "export DB_NAME=\"${DB_NAME:-sales}\"; if [[ ! \"$DB_NAME\" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]]; then echo 'Invalid DB_NAME'; exit 1; fi; export BACKUP_FILE=\"/tmp/backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).dump\"; pg_dump -h ${DB_HOST:-localhost} -U ${DB_USER:-postgres} -d $DB_NAME -F c -f $BACKUP_FILE && [ -s $BACKUP_FILE ] && echo 'Backup completed successfully' || echo 'Backup error'"

parameters.query: INSERT INTO backup_logs (filename, file_path, file_size, file_hash, backup_type, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, NOW())
